document: Existing cancer immunotherapy approaches suffer from poor tumor antigen specificity, systemic toxicity, and inefficient nucleic acid delivery to antigen-presenting cells. The present invention discloses an ionizable lipid nanoparticle (iLNP) formulation comprising a pH-sensitive ionizable lipid, a PEGylated phospholipid, cholesterol, and a helper lipid encapsulating tumor-associated antigen-encoding mRNA functionalized with a 5 foot cap analog and optimized untranslated regions for enhanced translational efficiency. Surface conjugation of tumor-homing peptides and anti-DEC-205 antibody fragments confers preferential endocytic uptake by dendritic cells within the tumor microenvironment. Following endosomal escape mediated by pH-dependent lipid conformational changes, the mRNA payload undergoes cytosolic translation, driving MHC-I antigen presentation and robust CD8+ cytotoxic T-lymphocyte priming. The disclosed formulation exhibits superior encapsulation efficiency exceeding 90%, reduced off-target immunogenicity, prolonged antigen expression, and statistically significant tumor regression in syngeneic murine models relative to prior lipoplex-based delivery systems.
